This"] # [doc = " section covers EVERY corpus doc, so nothing is dropped even if a doc is in no chapter (G2). {t}{lvl} " , id = html_escape (& node . anchor) , t = inline_html (node . title . as_deref () . unwrap_or ("")) ,)) ; for c in & node . children { render_node (c , depth + 1 , doc_anchor , buf) ; } buf . push_str ("
") ; } Payload :: Prose { text } => { if let Some (table) = render_table (text , & cid) { buf . push_str (& table) ; } else { buf . push_str (& format ! ("

{}

" , inline_html (text))) ; } } Payload :: Example { lang , source , checked , } => { let badge = if * checked { " ✓ checked" } else { " illustrative" } ; let inner = highlight :: highlight (lang , source) . unwrap_or_else (| | html_escape (source)) ; buf . push_str (& format ! (" " , lang = html_escape (lang) ,)) ; } Payload :: Xref { target } => { let (href , class) = match & target . resolution { XrefResolution :: Internal { anchor } => { let target_doc = anchor . split ("--") . next () . unwrap_or (anchor) ; let href = if target_doc == doc_anchor { format ! ("#{}" , html_escape (anchor)) } else { format ! ("{}.html#{}" , html_escape (target_doc) , html_escape (anchor)) } ; (href , "") } XrefResolution :: ExternalUrl | XrefResolution :: OutOfScope => { (html_escape (& target . raw) , "") } XrefResolution :: Dead { .. } | XrefResolution :: Unresolved => { (html_escape (& target . raw) , " class="unresolved"") } } ; buf . push_str (& format ! ("{t} " , t = html_escape (& target . raw) ,)) ; } Payload :: ApiItem { signature , summary } => { let h = depth . clamp (2 , 6) ; buf . push_str (& format ! ("

# [doc = " Render a GitHub-style pipe table (`| a | b |` with a `|---|---|` separator row) as a scroll-safe"] # [doc = " ``, keeping the node's `data-cid`. Returns `None` when `text` is not a well-formed table"] # [doc = " (then the caller renders a paragraph) — a robust check: the separator row must have the same cell"] # [doc = " count as the header, so ordinary prose with a stray dash is never mistaken for a table."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)` (with [`split_row`]/[`is_separator_cell`]) for white-box unit testing in"] # [doc = " `src/tests/html.rs`, not a downstream API."] pub (crate) fn render_table (text : & str , cid : & str) -> Option < String > { let lines : Vec < & str > = text . lines () . collect () ; if lines . len () < 2 { return None ; } if ! lines [0] . contains ('|') { return None ; } let header = split_row (lines [0]) ; if header . is_empty () { return None ; } let sep = split_row (lines [1]) ; if sep . len () != header . len () || ! sep . iter () . all (| c | is_separator_cell (c)) { return None ; } let mut out = format ! ("
") ; for cell in & header { out . push_str (& format ! ("" , inline_html (cell))) ; } out . push_str (" ") ; for line in & lines [2 ..] { if line . trim () . is_empty () { continue ; } out . push_str ("") ; for cell in split_row (line) { out . push_str (& format ! ("" , inline_html (& cell))) ; } out . push_str (" ") ; } out . push_str ("
{}
{}
") ; Some (out) }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Split a pipe-table row into trimmed cells (a single optional leading/trailing `|` is stripped)."] pub (crate) fn split_row (line : & str) -> Vec < String > { let t = line . trim () ; let t = t . strip_prefix ('|') . unwrap_or (t) ; let t = t . strip_suffix ('|') . unwrap_or (t) ; t . split ('|') . map (| c | c . trim () . to_owned ()) . collect () }

Emitting bare `trim(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Whether a separator cell is well-formed (`-`, optionally with alignment colons: `:--`, `--:`, `:-:`)."] pub (crate) fn is_separator_cell (cell : & str) -> bool { let c = cell . trim () ; ! c . is_empty () && c . contains ('-') && c . chars () . all (| ch | ch == '-' || ch == ':') }
